title: morphoFISH Protocols
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"beesv","text":"morphoFISH combines tissue clearing, submicron whole-brain imaging, and Expansion-Assisted Iterative Fluorescence In Situ Hybridization (EASI-FISH) to assign molecular identities to fully reconstructed neurons in the mouse brain. This protocol aggregates the morphoFISH procedures, including perfusion, tissue-clearing, and probing of transcripts.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3hhn1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"928b6","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"3a890","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"alt":"","guid":"FA7661DB002111F09F880A58A9FEAC02","height":94.9090909090909,"id":556495,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":null,\"entityMap\":null}","mime":"image/png","nestedSelector":null,"original_name":"morphoFISH-overview.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/files/rt9pbkr7p.png","shadow":false,"source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/s9qrbkr7p.jpg","webp_source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/s9qqbkr7p.webp","width":580}}}}

steps: Rinse 500ml glass bottle with RNAse AWAY.
Add 100 mL 1x PBS to beaker and warm in microwave for 00:01:30 Use caution glass bottle will be hot. Use appropriate protection.
Add 5 g agarose powder to warm 1x PBS, mix well by rotating the bottle.
Place glass bottle in 57 °C incubator for 02:00:00 or until agarose is dissolved

Submerge the brain in 25 mL, agarose in a 50mL conical tube for 00:15:00 37 °C Equilibrating the brain to agarose helps create a better seal around the brain when embedding in the mold. Store remaining agarose at 37 °C for later use.

Prepare 20x20 embedding mold by inserting two needles parallel to each other through the side of the mold about 2-3mm from the bottom. This creates a platform for the brain to rest while agarose is setting, ensuring brain remains in the middle of the mold.
Fill embedding mold with 15mL of 5% agarose

Quickly place the brain in the center of the mold resting on the needles and allow agarose to set 00:10:00 4 °C The agarose will turn slightly white in color and harden to the touch when it is completely set.

Equilibrate brain in 100 mL 30% DMSO for 72:00:00 a 40 rpm, Room temperature, protected from light. The agarose block will initially float in each index matching solution, but will gradually sink as it equilibrates to the solution.
Equilibrate brain in 100 mL 50:50 DMSO:Optiprep for 72:00:00 at 40 rpm, Room temperature, protected from light
Trim agarose block as needed
Glue block to specimen disc and fill imaging tray with 60:40 DMSO:Optiprep
Proceed to imaging as per Economo et al., 2016 ; Winnubst et al., 2018
Collect slice from imaging tray with sterile rod or brush into a 24-well plate. Briefly shining light into the imaging bath may assist in locating the brain slices for recovery

Rinse with 1 mL RNAse-free water for 00:05:00 at Room temperature to rinse off the DMSO. Trace amounts of DMSO left in the tissue may impact RNA transcript detection by hybridization chain reaction (HCR). Slices become opaque in aqueous solutions

Rinse 2x with 2 mL 70% EtOH for 00:05:00 at Room temperature XFP labeling (GFP, tdTomato, etc.) will become dim in 70% EtOH. Signal strength should recover once the brain slice is rehydrated in 1x PBS.

Store in 70% EtOH at 4 °C until further processing. Seal well plate with parafilm (or sealing film) to minimize evaporation. Brain slices are stable for at least 3 months, although it is recommended to proceed to RNA detection as quickly as possible Check ethanol level in the wells frequently to monitor evaporation. Replace 70% EtOH as needed.
morphoFISH Perfusion by Monique Copeland
Aqueous (SBiP) Delipidation for Whole Mouse Brain After morphoFISH Perfusion by Monique Copeland
Remove the agarose block from the mold by gently pulling the edges of the mold apart. Trim the block as desired and proceed immediately to index matching.
Expansion-assisted iterative fluorescence in situ hybridization (EASI-FISH) for morphoFISH by Mark Eddison
Reconstructing Complete Neurons in Whole Brains Using HortaCloud by Tiago A Ferreira
Replace with 100 mL 60:40 DMSO:Optiprep for 168:00:00 (7-10 days or until brain sinks to bottom) at 40 rpm, Room temperature, protected from light.
Equilibrate brain in 100 mL 60:40 DMSO:Optiprep for 72:00:00 at 40 rpm, Room temperature, protected from light.
creator: Monique Copeland
